content.problemStatement:
Chefland consists of NNN provinces (numbered 111 through NNN) connected by MMM bidirectional roads (numbered 111 through MMM). For each valid iii, the iii-th road connects provinces UiU_iUi​ and ViV_iVi​. It is possible to travel from each province to any other province using these roads.

For each valid iii, the annual income of the iii-th province is AiA_iAi​ and its population is BiB_iBi​. The per-capita income of some provinces is lower than that of some other provinces. For this reason, the king of Chefland wants to choose one or more provinces to keep in his kingdom and abandon the rest, in such a way that the per-capita income of the whole resulting kingdom, i.e. the sum of annual incomes of the chosen provinces divided by the sum of their populations, would be maximum possible. However, it should also be possible to travel from each of the chosen provinces to any other chosen province without visiting any of the abandoned provinces. Among all such ways to form the new kingdom, the king would prefer the number of chosen provinces to be as large as possible.

Can you help the king choose which provinces should form the new kingdom? If there are multiple solutions that maximise the per-capita income, you should choose one with the maximum number of chosen provinces. If there are still multiple solutions, you may choose any one.
constraints: 1≤Ui,Vi≤N1 \le U_i, V_i \le N1≤Ui​,Vi​≤N for each valid iii
Ui≠ViU_i \neq V_iUi​=Vi​ for each valid iii
there is at most one road between each pair of provinces
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.
MMM lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers UiU_iUi​ and ViV_iVi​.
content.outputFormat:
Print two lines.


The first of these lines should contain a single integer KKK denoting the number of provinces the king should choose.
The second line should contain KKK space-separated integers denoting these provinces.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
If the king chooses provinces 111 and 333, then the per-capita income is 10+25+1=2\frac{10 + 2}{5 + 1} = 25+110+2​=2. This is the only optimal solution.
